Recent estimates figure that, in the U.S., processed food typically travels over 1,300 miles andfresh produce over 1,500 milesbefore it\u2019s consumed...."}, {"title": "Food Miles and Buying Local and Seasonal | Orangeville Citizen", "url": "https://citizen.on.ca/food-miles-and-buying-local-and-seasonal/", "snippet": "December 23, 2021 -The term for that journey is \u201cfood ... the average North American meal travels between 2,400 to 4,000 kilometres (1,500 to 2,500 miles) from farm to table...."}, {"title": "Locally Sourced Produce \u2192 Term", "url": "https://lifestyle.sustainability-directory.com/term/locally-sourced-produce/", "snippet": "December 3, 2025 -The definition of \u201clocal\u201d itself is contested and context-dependent, ranging from a strict mileage radius (e.g.100-400 miles) to a more fluid concept of a \u201cfoodshed\u201d defined by ecological and cultural boundaries."}]
